codeReview.summary: The runtime and current Playwright spec now prove the canonical visible success confirmation after saving workspace settings, but the reviewed fix feature still fails because the worker handoff cites commit 7835272 even though that commit does not contain any settings-toast change. The actual assertion repair was already landed in commit 266727a, so this rerun/fix feature is not traceable to a scoped implementation unit that matches its description.

issues.description: The reviewed handoff attributes this fix to commit 7835272, but git show 7835272 contains only backend tracking regression work and no changes to the settings/profile Playwright spec or settings UI. The success-toast assertion repair at lines 64-65 was actually introduced earlier in commit 266727a, so the feature does not satisfy the skill requirement that a claimed code fix point to the commit that directly contains the scoped change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-test-worker skill already says rerun-only passes must not claim success for fix features and that handoff commitId must directly contain the scoped fix. This worker still handed off commit 7835272 after confirming the regression had already been fixed by commit 266727a.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill instructs workers to read .factory/library/user-testing.md, but this repo stores the file at .factory/library/user-testing.md under the repository root, not in the mission home directory. The worker transcript shows confusion around skill-path resolution and also reported a nonexistent external skill path as a discovered issue.

summary: Fail: the settings success toast is currently implemented and the test is correctly aligned to the visible Success!/Your workspace has been updated confirmation, but this rerun feature still does not provide a matching scoped fix commit because the cited handoff commit is unrelated to the actual repair.
